title: vigna unguiculata subsp. alba
intro: Vigna unguiculata subsp. alba, also known as white cowpea or white southern pea, is a subspecies of cowpea, a type of legume native to Africa. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, and its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Vigna unguiculata subsp. alba include its potential to improve cardiovascular health, reduce the risk of certain cancers, and aid in weight management. Vigna unguiculata subsp. alba can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or supplement, and is also used in traditional medicine in various forms, including dried powder and decoction.
